source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Subagent orphan cleanup (opencode 1-child variant; cleanup invariant identical for any N≥1)
notes: Parent opencode session spawns one foreground subagent via the `task` tool; once the child session row surfaces in the SQLite store with parent_id set (transcript_new + parent_linked) and the child turn is in flight (it runs `sleep 30`), the recipe SIGKILLs the live opencode process (the cwd-matched PID the daemon tracks), leaving the child orphaned rather than reaped by its own clean exit. Both the parent and the child are bound to the SAME opencode PID (the child subagent runs in-process), so when the daemon's PIDManager sees that PID vanish (CheckPIDLiveness → ESRCH) it records a process_exited for BOTH sessions via HandleProcessExit — the child's first (next liveness tick after the kill), then the parent's. The reap is process-exit-driven, NOT parser-driven: no SetState is issued, so NO spurious state_transition to working/waiting fires for the orphaned child during cleanup (issue #321 regression invariant). The child's last legitimate state is `working` (its in-flight turn at kill time); the #321 invariant is anchored on the child's process_exited and looks FORWARD, asserting the cleanup itself never resurrects the orphan into working/waiting. Delays are looser than claudecode because opencode's session-table poll runs less frequently than fswatcher inotify notifications and the local model is slow (~2 min for the parent to invoke the task tool).

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working

Subagent session row materializes in the SQLite store with parent_id set (the slow local model can take ~2 min after the parent turn starts to invoke the task tool, so this is a generous upper bound, not the observed offset)
Detector binds child.session_id → parent.session_id (opencode's subagent-spawn proof; the recorded session_id is the child's)

invariants: no state_transition to working for the orphaned child after cleanup
no state_transition to waiting for the orphaned child after cleanup
text: SIGKILL orphans the in-flight child; the sweep reaps it via process_exited and NEVER resurrects it into working/waiting (issue #321 invariant)
The same SIGKILL reaps the parent: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent session
